reasoning: Final iteration (3/3) must focus on Q3, which is 17.5x slower than DuckDB and accounts for 83% of total execution time. Parallelization (Priority 1) is the only technique that can deliver the required 3-4x speedup. Adding bloom filters (Priority 2) provides complementary 20-30% gains with low risk. Skipping Q1 parallelization to limit complexity on this final iteration.
focus_areas: Q3 multi-threaded parallel execution with morsel-driven parallelism
Q3 bloom filter for order_info hash table probe optimization
strategy_notes: This is the FINAL iteration (0 remaining). Conservative approach: focus all effort on Q3 parallelization (highest impact, medium risk) and bloom filters (low risk, stacks well). Combined expected improvement: 3.6-4.6x speedup on Q3 (2369ms → 515-660ms), closing gap from 17.5x to 3.8-4.9x vs DuckDB. Q1 and Q6 are already acceptable (1.25x gap and beating DuckDB respectively). Implementation risks: thread synchronization, race conditions. Mitigation: use thread-local aggregation to avoid lock contention. Test thoroughly with different thread counts (2, 4, 8).
notes: CRITICAL: Since this is the final iteration, prioritize correctness over performance. Test parallelization thoroughly before bloom filter addition. If parallelization shows any correctness issues (wrong results, crashes), the bloom filter can be implemented as a safer fallback optimization. Recommended thread count: start with 4 threads (typical CPU count), then scale to hardware_concurrency if stable. Morsel size tuning: start with 50K rows, profile and adjust. Ensure thread-local states are aligned to 64-byte boundaries to prevent false sharing.
